nodes.context: Second-largest yakuza group in Japan, a confederation of smaller gangs.
Main rival of Sumiyoshi-kai, largest yakuza group in Japan.
Yakuza group whose head was found dead, raising tensions with Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: Structurally, the Sumiyoshi-kai differs from its main rival, the Yamaguchi-gumi.
Police stated that they were probing for possible links between the murder and a series of shootings between the Sumiyoshi-kai and the Yamaguchi-gumi in neighboring Tochigi Prefecture.
edges.context: Shootings occurred between Sumiyoshi-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: Within hours the offices of the Yamaguchi-gumi were fired upon in retribution.
edges.context: Sumiyoshi-kai retaliated against Yamaguchi-gumi after a boss was shot.

edges.evidence_quote: raising tensions between the Sumiyoshi-kai and the Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.context: Tensions rose between Sumiyoshi-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi after a suicide.
